arguments: P1 (delete Rust) loses no security. Verified: zero cryptography executes in Rust — src/lib/wasm/crypto.ts implements all AES-256-GCM/PBKDF2/CSPRNG in WebCrypto and Rust calls back into window.webcryptoEncrypt/Decrypt via setupCryptoBridge(); pkg/mindline.js imports only those JS functions. Timing safety: browser WebCrypto is native constant-time AES (AES-NI/BoringSSL), strictly better than any wasm software AES would be. Memory safety: wasm linear memory is JS-readable and keys already cross the FFI boundary as raw Uint8Arrays; replacing raw-byte handling with non-extractable CryptoKey objects (the pattern already used in connection.ts:177-190) is a strict improvement. The 'Rust = security' framing was always a façade.

P2's app-layer AES-GCM over DTLS is NOT wasteful double encryption — it is the only E2E in the design. WebRTC DTLS uses self-signed certificates authenticated solely by a=fingerprint in SDP, and offers/answers transit the signaling server (signaling-server.js:327-329) with zero fingerprint verification anywhere in src/lib/p2p/ (verified by grep). A malicious/compromised signaling operator can rewrite fingerprints and MITM the entire 'P2P' connection invisibly; TURN does not fix this either. Encryption keyed by a fragment secret the server never sees is the only defense. Performance cost is noise (WebCrypto GCM does hundreds of MB/s; chat messages and 16KB media chunks are trivial).

Key-in-URL-fragment is sound versus servers and has strong precedent: Excalidraw (#room=id,key), CryptPad (keys in #), Firefox Send (#key), Jitsi Meet E2EE. Fragments are never sent in HTTP requests, never appear in Referer headers (spec strips fragments regardless of referrer policy), and never reach SvelteKit SSR, Cloudflare, or the signaling server. The 256-bit random key is full-entropy, so HKDF (no PBKDF2) is correct.

However, fragment keys have two documented residual leaks the plan is silent on: (a) browser history stores the full URL and Chrome/Safari sync uploads history/open-tabs to Google/iCloud — without an E2E sync passphrase the room key lands on those servers (CryptPad documents this exact risk); (b) the invite link IS the key, so whatever channel carries the invite (WhatsApp/Slack/email) holds both rendezvous and key — inherent to no-accounts link sharing, but PRODUCT.md principle 3 ('trust through honesty') requires disclosing it. Neither refutes P2; both demand explicit decisions.

Fragment-key security presupposes no hostile JS can read location.hash — currently violated in spirit: the app loads Google Fonts at runtime from a privacy product, _headers ships a stale CSP whitelisting cdn.tailwindcss.com, and there is no Referrer-Policy or strict CSP (verified: no referrer/CSP handling in src/app.html). XSS or any future third-party script steals the room key. CSP hardening is a prerequisite of P2, not a nice-to-have.

P4 (drop relay) is directionally right but its stated justification is broken: 'TURN covers NAT-blocked cases' is false in the current tree. The only configured TURN is openrelay.metered.ca free tier with static public credentials (config.ts:11-22) — a discontinued/throttled shared service — and getP2PConfig sets forceRelay on mobile networks (config.ts:154), making that dead TURN the SOLE path for cellular users. Dropping the WS relay before provisioning real TURN regresses mobile availability, violating 'ultra-reliable, mobile-first'. Note also the post-ICE-failure relay path is already dead code (removePeer wipes allKnownPeers before relayPeers.add — connection.ts:761-772 vs 1304), so the actual availability loss from removal is smaller than it appears.

Dropping the relay as-built is a genuine security win today: the ECDH key exchange is unauthenticated raw P-256 public keys routed through the very server being distrusted (connection.ts:128-198, no pinning, no fingerprint), and connection.ts:560-561 accepts PLAINTEXT relay payloads 'for backward compatibility' while the server controls fromId — so the operator can both read relayed traffic via MITM and inject arbitrary messages right now. But note: once P2 ships, a relay carrying fragment-key-encrypted payloads would no longer be content-MITM-able — the enduring reasons to drop it are simplicity, the 16KB maxPayload (useless for P5 media), and making 'messages never transit our servers' literally true.

P2 as stated covers the wire but not rest: chat history persists as plaintext JSON in localStorage (messages.rs:524-580 pattern, to be reimplemented in TS per P1) and P5 plans IndexedDB blob storage with no mention of encryption. Room keys are also currently stored as plaintext JSON (crypto.rs:243-265, mindline_encryption_key_*). Shipping 'real E2E' while history and media sit plaintext on disk repeats the exact honesty failure the audit found in the README.

P2 lacks protocol-level specifics that distinguish sound from subtly broken multi-party GCM: multiple senders sharing one AES-GCM key with random 96-bit IVs is statistically fine at chat volume but needs discipline for media chunking (thousands of encryptions per transfer); there is no AAD binding (sender, message type, message id, chunk index) to prevent cross-context replay/reflection/reordering of ciphertexts; and nothing authenticates room membership — the signaling operator learns every room ID (cleartext join messages) and can join as a silent peer who receives ciphertext and presence metadata. A key-confirmation handshake is needed so key-less joiners surface as unverified rather than as normal peers.

P3 has an unstated identity problem: polite/impolite roles for perfect negotiation derive from server-assigned client IDs, but the server assigns a fresh UUID per WebSocket connection — and P3/P4 explicitly keep DataChannels alive across signaling drops. After a signaling reconnect every surviving peer has a stale ID, breaking role determinism and creating ghost/duplicate peers unless stable client-generated peer IDs (ideally HMAC-authenticated with a key derived from the fragment secret) are mapped onto ephemeral signaling IDs.
amendments: P4 sequencing: make real TURN a hard prerequisite of relay removal — self-hosted coturn or paid TURN with ephemeral HMAC credentials issued by the signaling server, including turns: on 443 for restrictive networks — and remove forceRelay/iceTransportPolicy:'relay' on cellular (let ICE try host/srflx first). Ship TURN, measure connection success rate, then delete the relay.

P2: extend encryption to rest, not just wire. Derive a storage key via HKDF from the fragment key (domain-separated info string) and encrypt localStorage history and all IndexedDB media blobs with it. Consequence to embrace: history is unreadable without the link, which matches PRODUCT.md 'history never leaves the devices'.

P2: specify the protocol details — HKDF domain separation per purpose (messages / typing / sync / media / storage); AAD binding every ciphertext to {roomId, senderId, messageType, messageId/chunkIndex}; per-sender nonce strategy (random 96-bit is acceptable for chat, use counter-with-sender-prefix or per-transfer HKDF subkeys for media chunks); and a key-confirmation handshake after DataChannel open so peers lacking the fragment key are shown as unverified/blocked instead of appearing as normal participants.

P2: make an explicit, documented decision on fragment persistence: either keep #k= in the URL (simple, refresh/share-from-address-bar works, but key syncs to Google/iCloud via browser history unless user has E2E sync) or read-then-strip via history.replaceState with sessionStorage backup (no history leak, but address bar is no longer the invite). Add a visible 'this link is the key — share it only with people you want in the room' disclosure per the trust-through-honesty principle; optionally show a short emoji/word key fingerprint for out-of-band verification.

P2/P6 prerequisite: lock down the page that holds the key — strict CSP (no third-party script sources), self-host Inter via fontsource (drop Google Fonts runtime fetch), set Referrer-Policy, and purge the stale _headers CSP whitelisting cdn.tailwindcss.com. Without this, XSS or a future third-party script reads location.hash and the whole E2E story collapses.

P3: introduce stable client-generated peer identity (per-session UUID or key-derived, HMAC'd with an HKDF-derived auth key so it can't be spoofed by the signaling operator) mapped to ephemeral server-assigned signaling IDs; derive polite/impolite roles and peer dedup from the stable ID so DataChannels genuinely survive signaling reconnects.

P3/P5: split traffic across two DataChannels — fully reliable ordered (no maxRetransmits) for chat/sync/edits/acks, and unordered maxRetransmits:0 for typing snapshots — replacing today's single ordered+maxRetransmits:3 channel that silently drops chat and head-of-line-blocks on typing bursts.

P5: encrypt each chunk with AAD {transferId, chunkIndex, totalChunks} and a manifest carrying the whole-file hash so truncation/reordering/cross-transfer splicing fails closed; document iOS Safari's 7-day ITP storage eviction and request persistent storage (navigator.storage.persist) for IndexedDB blobs.

P4: alongside origin checks, only trust cf-connecting-ip/x-forwarded-for behind a known proxy, add a per-room peer cap (~8, mesh degrades anyway), and fix the leave handler that never clears the server-side currentRoom binding. Also delete the protocol-incompatible prototype signaling-server/ directory as part of this phase.

P1: when porting persistence to TypeScript, import the fragment key once and hold only non-extractable CryptoKey handles at runtime; never re-persist raw key bytes (today's mindline_encryption_key_* plaintext localStorage pattern must not be carried over).

Docs: as part of P2, rewrite README/PRIVACY/CLAUDE.md security claims to match the new reality exactly, including the residual risks (invite-channel holds the key; browser history sync; metadata visible to signaling/TURN operators: room IDs, IPs, timing, peer counts). The audit showed false claims are this product's core credibility risk.

arguments: P1 (delete Rust/WASM) does NOT close a native-port door, because that door is painted on. README.md:275 promises 'Native Mobile Apps - iOS and Android apps with same Rust core', but src-rust/crypto.rs:10-21 shows the Rust 'crypto' is a wasm_bindgen shim calling window.webcryptoEncrypt/webcryptoDecrypt (JS WebCrypto does the actual AES-GCM), persistence calls web_sys::window/localStorage (crypto.rs:244-300), and P2P never existed in Rust (no p2p.rs; lib.rs confirms it moved to TS). Nothing in src-rust is portable to native; a native client would rewrite all of it regardless. The reusable asset for future ports is a protocol spec, not this code. Also PRODUCT.md (the authoritative rebuild doc) targets people who 'won't install anything' — web/PWA-first, no native roadmap.

P4 as stated is REFUTABLE on its premise: 'TURN covers NAT-blocked cases' is false today. The only configured TURN is the free Open Relay Project with shared public credentials (src/lib/p2p/config.ts:11-22, openrelay.metered.ca / 'openrelayproject'), a best-effort free service, and the production env override that could replace it is broken (inject-env.js writes to dist/, which the Cloudflare build never publishes — per the analysis report, signaling-deploy section). Worse, config.ts:154 sets forceRelay=true on cellular networks, so on mobile — the product's primary platform — ALL traffic is forced through that free TURN. Dropping the WS relay before standing up operated TURN regresses reliability for symmetric-NAT/CGNAT users, directly contradicting 'ultra-reliable, mobile-first'. Order of operations matters: real TURN first, then drop relay.

P2's no-accounts model is compatible with confidentiality but NOT, by itself, with sender authenticity — and the plan is silent on this. With one shared fragment key, every payload is encrypted but senderId/senderName remain self-asserted fields trusted on receipt; edit/delete ownership checks compare attacker-supplied senderId against stored sender_id (src/lib/p2p/handlers.ts:366-439 per report), so any room member can forge, edit, or delete messages as anyone else, and captured ciphertexts can be replayed (random-IV GCM has no replay binding). This is fixable without accounts via per-session keypairs (TOFU), but if left unaddressed the product's 'Trust through honesty' principle (PRODUCT.md) is violated again — encrypted-but-forgeable is a new gap between claims and code. Full authenticity against a malicious key-holder is impossible by design in a capability-URL model and must be documented, not implied away.

P5's presence-centric P2P-only media is NOT too limited to bother — PRODUCT.md explicitly embraces synchronous presence ('Both peers are typically online at the same time... lean into that instead of apologizing for it'; 'Presence over history'). 'Both online' is already a hard precondition for chat itself in this product, so media inherits no new constraint. But P5 is the plan's only scope EXPANSION (everything else is deletion/repair) and bundles four features (files, photos, voice, video capture); the hero feature is live typing, not media. Unphased, it is the biggest schedule and bug risk.

P3 as written understates two reliability-critical defects it must own: (a) the single DataChannel is created with maxRetransmits:3 (verified at connection.ts:629), making chat/edits/sync-responses silently droppable on lossy mobile networks — 'perfect negotiation' alone does not fix message loss; (b) sync ships entire room history as one JSON DataChannel message from every peer (O(N^2), exceeds ~64-256KB cross-browser SCTP limits and silently fails per the p2p-webrtc analysis). Both are 'ultra-reliable' blockers independent of negotiation logic.

P1's blast radius extends beyond src/lib/wasm and src-rust: verify:rust/verify:web/verify:ai gates and build-wasm are wired into package.json:16,31-33 and CI; tests/e2e/persistence.spec.ts:229-247 asserts WASM-era artifacts (mindline_encryption_key_* in localStorage); CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md (src-rust override), README architecture sections, and 'wasm'/'encryption' package keywords all describe the deleted layer. A plan that deletes the layer but not its scaffolding leaves CI red and docs lying — the exact 'claims must match code' failure being fixed.

The 'smaller scope' challenge fails as a refutation: P1+P4 are net-negative-code deletions, P2 is ~small (HKDF + encrypt/decrypt wrappers around an existing correct AES-GCM/12-byte-IV pattern), P3 replaces ~400 lines of collision machinery with ~50 lines of perfect negotiation. The plan as a whole shrinks the codebase while making the headline claim true. The only candidate for cutting is within P5 (video/voice capture), handled by phasing, not by refuting the plan.
amendments: P4 (reorder, do not drop as-is): stand up operated TURN BEFORE removing the WebSocket relay — self-hosted coturn (~$5/mo on Fly/Railway) or Cloudflare Calls TURN, with short-lived HMAC credentials vended by the signaling server, including turns: on 443/tcp for restrictive networks. Simultaneously fix the broken config pipeline (replace scripts/inject-env.js + window.MINDLINE_ENV with build-time VITE_ vars; fix the 1-year-immutable /*.js cache header in _headers) so TURN/signaling URLs are actually configurable in production. Re-evaluate forceRelay-on-cellular (config.ts:154) once real TURN exists — let ICE choose.

P2 (add sender authenticity): generate a per-session ECDSA P-256 or Ed25519 keypair per peer, announce the public key inside the first encrypted payload after join, sign message/edit/delete/reaction envelopes, and verify edit/delete ownership against the signing key (not self-asserted senderId). Dedupe by message ID to kill replay. This needs no accounts (TOFU per session) and closes the forged-edit/delete hole in handlers.ts:366-439.

P2 (key hygiene and honesty): encrypt localStorage/IndexedDB history at rest with an HKDF-derived storage key (or explicitly document plaintext-at-rest); after first load, persist the fragment key locally and document that capability URLs leak via browser history/sync and via whatever channel shares the link; keep distinct HKDF info labels per channel (chat, drafts, sync, media, storage). Do not re-publish README/PRIVACY E2E claims until P2 actually ships — fix the docs in the P1 commit.

P3 (extend scope by two items): split traffic across two DataChannels — fully reliable ordered (no maxRetransmits) for chat/edits/acks/sync, unordered lossy for typing snapshots — replacing the current maxRetransmits:3 channel at connection.ts:629; and rewrite sync as delta-based (honor lastSync), responder-to-requester only, chunked under 16KB. Both are prerequisites for 'ultra-reliable' and for P5's transfer engine.

P5 (phase it): ship the chunked encrypted transfer engine + file/photo send first (16-64KB binary chunks, bufferedAmountLowThreshold backpressure, manifest + resume offsets, IndexedDB blobs); voice notes second (MediaRecorder opus, fits the 'live wire' framing); defer video capture to a later milestone. Frame transfers as a 'live handoff' with honest paused/waiting-for-peer states, per the presence-centric product position.

P1 (include the scaffolding): in the same workstream, rewrite verify:* gates (drop verify:rust, build-wasm), update CI and cloud_build.sh, fix tests/e2e/persistence.spec.ts assertions on mindline_encryption_key_*, update CLAUDE.md/README/AGENTS.md (remove 'Rust core' identity, README:275 native-apps-same-Rust-core roadmap line, 'wasm' keywords), and delete the dead deployment sprawl the analysis flagged (signaling-server/ prototype directory, nginx.conf, ecosystem.config.js, _redirects SPA fallback).

New deliverable (preserves the only real 'future ports' door): write a short PROTOCOL.md specifying the wire format — fragment-key encoding, HKDF labels, AES-GCM envelope, message types, signing scheme, signaling protocol — so any future native client can interoperate. This replaces the fictional 'same Rust core' reuse story with a real one.

P4 (cheap hardenings while touching the server): Origin allowlist on the WS upgrade (not just HTTP), per-room peer cap (~8, mesh degrades past that anyway), trust cf-connecting-ip/x-forwarded-for only behind the known proxy, and fix the 'leave' handler that never clears the server-side room binding.

design_concepts.name: Iron Gall
design_concepts.scene_sentence: Two people lean over the same sheet of warm paper in a quiet, well-lit room, watching each other's handwriting darken as the ink dries.
design_concepts.theme: Both, light-primary. The scene sentence forces light: a well-lit room and a sheet of paper cannot be authored on a dark ground, and PRODUCT.md's own tone line ("a quiet, well-lit room") confirms it. Light ("Paper") is the designed theme; dark ("Inkwell") is the same room after the lamp is dimmed: surfaces become deep blue-black iron-gall ink, text becomes paper-colored, the wet-ink accent lightens to stay legible. Dark is mandatory because this is a phones-first, presence-centric product used in bed and on night trains, but it is a translation of the light theme, never the identity. This is also the anti-slop move: every "encrypted chat" defaults to dark-first; Mindline deliberately is not.
design_concepts.color_strategy: Restrained, with exactly one surface earning Committed: the live draft. Rationale: the product's stated delight budget lives entirely in the live-typing moment, so color must be spent there and almost nowhere else. The system is two materials: warm paper neutrals (hue 84, chroma 0.005-0.014) and cool iron-gall ink neutrals (hue 272, chroma 0.008-0.018) used for all text, the natural warm-paper/cool-ink tension of real writing. One accent: wet-ink indigo oklch(0.45 0.105 272), reserved for liveness and intent: the in-stream draft text, the breathing caret, presence dots, focus rings, the primary action. Sent bubbles get a faint ink wash, not a filled black slab. Semantic inks stay in the same material register: moss ink for connected, amber ink for reconnecting, madder ink for destructive. No gradients, no glass, no padlock green. The committed surface is the wet-ink draft block: accent text on an accent-tinted wash, the most colored thing on screen by design, so "someone is writing to you right now" is the visual loudest moment in the product.
design_concepts.palette: LIGHT (Paper): background oklch(0.962 0.007 84); raised surface/card+popover oklch(0.978 0.005 84); foreground (dried ink) oklch(0.225 0.018 272); muted-foreground oklch(0.48 0.014 272); hairline border/input oklch(0.885 0.008 84); secondary/muted wash oklch(0.93 0.009 84); hover accent wash oklch(0.915 0.012 84); primary/accent (wet ink) oklch(0.45 0.105 272) with foreground oklch(0.972 0.005 84); sent-message wash oklch(0.925 0.014 272); draft text oklch(0.45 0.105 272), idle-draft at /0.55, draft ground oklch(0.45 0.105 272 / 0.07); ring oklch(0.45 0.105 272); success/connected (moss ink) oklch(0.56 0.09 150); warning/reconnecting (amber ink) oklch(0.65 0.12 75); destructive (madder ink) oklch(0.52 0.16 25) with foreground oklch(0.972 0.005 84). DARK (Inkwell): background oklch(0.185 0.014 272); card/popover oklch(0.215 0.015 272); foreground (paper ink) oklch(0.92 0.008 84); muted-foreground oklch(0.68 0.012 84); border/input oklch(0.275 0.015 272); secondary/muted oklch(0.245 0.016 272); accent hover oklch(0.27 0.018 272); primary oklch(0.74 0.085 268) with foreground oklch(0.185 0.014 272); sent wash oklch(0.265 0.022 272); draft text oklch(0.78 0.075 268), ground at oklch(0.74 0.085 268 / 0.09); ring oklch(0.74 0.085 268); success oklch(0.72 0.10 150); warning oklch(0.78 0.11 80); destructive oklch(0.68 0.14 25). Never pure black or white anywhere; every neutral carries either the paper hue (84) or the ink hue (272). Timestamps move from 10px/70%-opacity to 12px full muted-foreground to clear contrast floors.
design_concepts.typography: Two families, both self-hosted via fontsource (OFL; removes the current fonts.googleapis.com runtime leak flagged in the audit). PROSE: Newsreader Variable (with italic axis, optical size ~16) for everything a human says: message content, live drafts, composer text, the landing promise line. The serif is the "ink"; its true italic is the wet-ink voice. CHROME: Inter Variable for everything the system says: names, timestamps (tabular-nums), buttons, statuses, labels. Type IS the hierarchy: a reader instantly parses serif = person, sans = machine, italic = still being written, roman = said. Fixed rem scale, ratio ~1.2, no fluid clamp: 0.75 / 0.875 / 1.0625 / 1.25 / 1.5 / 1.875rem. Message prose at 1.0625rem (17px) Newsreader 400, line-height 1.5; names Inter 600 at 0.75rem; timestamps Inter 400 0.75rem tabular; buttons Inter 500 0.875rem; landing display Newsreader 500 at 1.875rem (2.25rem desktop), not bold. Weights total: Newsreader 400/500 + italics, Inter 400/500/600. Letterspacing: small-caps-style tracking (0.06em, uppercase) only on tiny labels like day dividers and the "or join" rule.
design_concepts.layout: Shell: 100dvh grid of rows [auto 1fr auto], viewport-fit=cover added to app.html, visualViewport listener keeps the composer pinned above the iOS keyboard; this fixes the audit's dead safe-area/100vh findings as part of the redesign, not after it. HEADER: the current two stacked bars (global header + room status bar) merge into one 48px room bar with a bottom hairline: left = small wordmark glyph + editable room/your-name; right = presence cluster (peer count + breathing connection dot with visible text label, since tooltips are unreachable on touch) + one Invite button that always copies/shares the full URL (the bare-UUID copy pill dies). Tapping the presence cluster opens a bottom sheet on mobile (popover on desktop) with connection detail and reconnect, replacing tooltip-only meaning. ROOM: a single centered column, max-width 42rem (~65ch of 17px serif), the editorial measure; px-4 on mobile. Vertical rhythm on a 4px base: 4px inside a sender group, 16px between groups, 32px around day dividers (centered hairline + small-caps date, an editorial section break, not a pill). No cards anywhere in the stream. COMPOSER: hairline top rule, auto-growing textarea (1 to 6 rows, fixing the audit's no-grow finding), reserved left slot for the future attach (+) control, 44px circular wet-ink send disc in the right-thumb zone, padding-bottom max(12px, safe-area-inset-bottom). LANDING: the card is deleted. A type-only composition: wordmark, then the promise set as a live specimen (a sentence types itself in wet-ink italic and dries to roman, demonstrating the product without a screenshot), then "Start a room" as the single primary action and the join field as a quiet secondary row beneath a hairline. One screen, zero chrome, seconds-to-talking. Motion everywhere: 150-250ms, ease-out-expo cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), state-conveying only, fully disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_concepts.hero_typing_treatment: The remote draft leaves the strip above the composer and becomes a first-class WET-INK ENTRY inside the message stream, rendered exactly where the sent message will land. Lifecycle: (1) APPEAR: when a peer's draft arrives, a draft block enters at the bottom of the stream in the peer's normal left-aligned position: byline (Inter 600 name + a breathing moss dot, 2400ms ease cycle, the "breath") above text set in Newsreader Italic in wet-ink accent at full strength, sitting on the faint --wash-draft ground (a tint wash, explicitly no side-stripe border). (2) FLOW: each newly received character fades in over 150ms ease-out-expo from 0 opacity with a 0.3px blur resolving to sharp, ink soaking into paper; a hairline caret after the last character pulses on the breath cycle. The block grows naturally and autoscroll keeps it pinned only when the reader is already at bottom (fixing the force-scroll bug). (3) HOLD: the draft never vanishes while text exists in the peer's composer (the 3s deletion in drafts.ts goes away; that behavior contradicted radical transparency). After ~6s of stillness it settles to 55% opacity and the caret stops pulsing: breath held, not gone. An empty-but-focused composer shows only byline + breathing dot. (4) DRY: on send, the block transforms IN PLACE into the real message: italic crossfades to roman, wet-ink accent darkens to foreground ink, the wash fades out, 200ms, zero layout jump because the draft node becomes the message node. The ink dries; that transition IS the product's signature moment. (5) HONESTY: your own composer text is set in the same wet-ink italic serif, so you see exactly what they see, and a one-time line under the composer states in small italic: "People in this room see your words as you write them." Reduced-motion users get instant character appearance and a static presence dot; the staging still reads through type and color alone.
design_concepts.component_notes: MessageBubble becomes MessageBlock: received messages lose the filled bubble entirely (transparent on paper, hanging Inter byline + Newsreader prose, consecutive messages grouped under one byline); sent messages keep right alignment for chat familiarity but swap the black slab for the faint cool --wash-sent at radius 10px. select-none is removed (text becomes selectable) and Copy joins Edit/Delete in both MessageActions and LongPressMenu. The max-h-400px inner scroll is dropped. Delivery checks stay (Signal-familiar) but the broken get()-inside-$derived read is replaced with a reactive store subscription so they actually update; tooltip detail moves into the long-press sheet on touch. MessageInput: textarea auto-grows 1-6 rows, typed text set in Newsreader Italic wet-ink (your side of the transparency contract), send becomes a 44px accent disc, a disabled-for-now attach slot reserves the media future, safe-area padding becomes real once viewport-fit=cover lands. DraftIndicator is deleted; the same drafts store feeds a new in-stream LiveDraft component (see hero treatment). TypingIndicator's three-dot bounce is replaced by the single breathing dot. ConnectionStatus: badge-plus-tooltip becomes a presence cluster in the merged header: breathing moss dot + visible label (Connected / Reconnecting 3s / On your own); amber pulse while reconnecting, static gray when local; full detail and the Reconnect action live in a tap-to-open bottom sheet (desktop popover), never tooltip-only. Landing page: Card/CardHeader composition is removed in favor of the type-only specimen layout; the join field keeps its robust URL/ID parsing; name prompt happens inline at create/join instead of silently defaulting to Anonymous. Header merges with the room status bar into one 48px bar and a single Invite action that always shares the full URL. AppShell: the blocking WASM spinner gate disappears with the WASM layer per the analysis verdict; the shell paints instantly with self-hosted fonts (font-display: swap with size-adjusted fallbacks) and 100dvh. The leave-confirmation dialog is dropped (reversible action); toasts are reserved for failures only.
